title: Puerto Banus Luxury Yacht Charter Day: Costs & Tips 2026
html:
Puerto Banus on the Costa del Sol is synonymous with superyachts and glamour, but a day charter on the Mediterranean is more accessible than you might think, with prices from around €1,500 for a small group on a crewed motor yacht. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local owners and brokers to find the ideal vessel for your party.

How much does it cost to rent a luxury yacht in Puerto Banus for a day?

A private crewed day charter in high season starts from €3,500 for an 18m yacht and rises to over €10,000 for a 30m superyacht. Prices vary by size, age, and included extras such as jet skis or water toys. The base cost typically covers the yacht, fuel for a standard itinerary, crew, and insurance.

For a realistic budget, consider a shared tour or a smaller motorboat. A half-day trip on a 10m Axopar 28 costs around €800 to €1,200, while a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests might be €2,500 to €4,000. All prices are in EUR and, as with any yacht charter in Puerto Banus, fuel and crew gratuity are often extra.

Which boat to rent for a day in Puerto Banus?

The best boat for you depends on group size and whether you want speed, space, or luxury. For a day trip along the coast, consider one of the following types.

  1. Maiora 26m open, ideal for a stylish party of up to 12; from €6,500 per day. See the full spec on the Maiora 26m charter page.
  2. Azimut 58 flybridge, spacious saloon and sun pads for 10 guests; from €4,000 per day.
  3. Sunseeker Predator 74, sporty design for 8 guests; from €5,500 per day.
  4. Lagoon 50 catamaran, stable and roomy for up to 16 guests; from €3,800 per day.
  5. Axopar 28, quick and nimble for 6 guests, half-day from €800.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus?

Most day charters depart from Puerto Banus marina, but you can also start from nearby Marbella or Estepona marinas. The sheltered marina at Puerto Banus sits behind the famous breakwater and offers fuelling and provisioning. Within 15 nautical miles you can reach Marbella's lighthouse, the secluded bay of Cabopino, or the Punta de Baños cove. For a longer run, head to Gibraltar or the African coast.

Other recognised departure points include Puerto José Banús (the official name), Marina Marbella, and Sotogrande to the north. Each offers its own charm and access to the Mediterranean.

Costs broken down

Here is a quick price guide for day charters in Puerto Banus, always in EUR.

  • Shared group tour on a 12m yacht: €150-€250 per person
  • Small motorboat (8-10m) half-day: €600-€1,200
  • Catamaran full-day for 12 guests: €2,500-€4,000
  • Private crewed motor yacht (18-30m): €3,500-€12,000

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

For crewed charters you do not need a licence; the professional skipper holds all required qualifications. If you plan to drive a bareboat (no skipper) on the Mediterranean, the Spanish authorities require a valid recreational boating licence depending on boat size and power. The operator will confirm your specific licence needs at booking. For bareboat rentals under 8m, a basic certificate may suffice, but always check.

For a day trip without a licence, the simplest option is to charter a yacht with a skipper. They know the best anchorages, like the bay of Cala de Mijas, and handle all navigation.

When is the best season to charter a yacht in Puerto Banus?

High season runs from June through September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months. The wind is generally light, although the thermal Poniente breeze picks up in the afternoon. Shoulder months (May and October) offer calm seas, fewer crowds, and lower prices. Winter charters are possible but the sea can be choppier and some vessels are in dry dock.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150 to €12,000 per day (EUR)
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Maximum guests: 12 on most standard charters
  • Currency: Euro (EUR)
  • Licence needed: No for crewed; yes for bareboat

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I charter a yacht for a sunset cruise in Puerto Banus?

Yes, many operators offer 2-3 hour sunset cruises departing around 19:00, costing from €300 to €600 for a small motorboat.

What safety equipment is provided on a crewed charter?

The yacht will carry life jackets for all guests, a first aid kit, fire extinguishers, and a VHF radio; the crew will give a safety briefing before departure.

Is food and drink included in the charter price?

On most crewed charters a basic menu (e.g., tapas, soft drinks, water) is included; premium catering and bar packages cost extra.

Can I book a last-minute day charter in Puerto Banus?

Yes, you can often book a day or two ahead via BoatHire24, especially in shoulder months, but high-season availability requires earlier booking.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. Whether you are planning a private day on the Mediterranean or a multi-day superyacht itinerary, the BoatHire24 marketplace lets you compare verified listings and book directly with owners. If you own a yacht in Puerto Banus, you can list your boat and start earning income from charter guests.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
